title: Quiz: two users booking the last seat at once
prompt_md: Two users try to book the last available seat at the exact same moment. Both requests read "1 seat left" and both proceed. How do you prevent a double-booking?
options: Trust the frontend to disable the button once one user is mid-checkout
Add a 200ms random delay to each request so they don't arrive simultaneously
Use an updated_at timestamp and let the later write win, since it has the freshest data
Run the read-and-update inside a database transaction using row locking (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE), or do a single atomic conditional UPDATE that only succeeds if seats are still available
explanation_md: This is a **race condition** on shared state, and the fix is making the check-and-decrement **atomic**. Wrap it in a **transaction** and use **pessimistic locking** — `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` locks the seat row so the second request blocks until the first commits and then sees zero seats. Equivalently, a single **atomic conditional UPDATE** (`UPDATE seats SET remaining = remaining - 1 WHERE remaining > 0`) lets the database serialize the writes for you. A timestamp 'last write wins' is exactly the wrong instinct — both writes still succeed and you've sold the seat twice; client-side guards and random delays don't enforce correctness on the server.
